title: Goldfussia chaffanjonii
taxonomy: Goldfussia chaffanjonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. It is native to Madagascar. Goldfussia chaffanjonii is a member of the genus Goldfussia, which is a group of plants that are often referred to as the 'goldfussia' or 'gold dust plants'. This plant is a type of shrub that is known for its small white flowers and glossy leaves. Goldfussia chaffanjonii is also sometimes referred to as Plectranthus chaffanjonii.
